skill.name: raffle-winner-picker
skill.description: Picks random winners from lists, spreadsheets, or Google Sheets for giveaways, raffles, and contests. It supports exclusions, runner-ups, and transparent result summaries.
skill.summary: Pick fair raffle winners from lists, files, or Google Sheets.
skill.author: ComposioHQ

security_audit.summary: Static alerts for Ruby backticks, weak cryptography, and network reconnaissance are false positives after reviewing SKILL.md. The skill contains Markdown instructions only, with no executable code, prompt injection, or malicious intent. The remaining concern is low privacy risk because raffle data may include names, emails, and spreadsheet rows.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are Markdown prompt examples and sample output, not executable Ruby or shell code. No command execution instruction or script is present in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are inside fenced Markdown examples for user prompts and displayed output. There is no Ruby code, shell invocation, or executable file in the skill.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Alert Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The flagged description says the skill picks random winners. It does not name or implement a weak cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is plain descriptive metadata, not code. No MD5, SHA1, insecure PRNG implementation, or cryptographic operation appears at the flagged line.

low_findings.title: Network Reconnaissance Alert Is False Positive
low_findings.description: The flagged line contains the phrase Transparent process in a fairness feature list. It does not describe scanning, probing, or network access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact context is a feature bullet about transparency. I found no network reconnaissance commands, host discovery logic, or similar intent in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: User Data Handling Requires Care
low_findings.description: The skill is intended to process spreadsheets, Google Sheets, names, and email addresses. This is legitimate for raffles, but users should avoid exposing unnecessary personal data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented workflow includes winner details such as names, emails, and spreadsheet rows. This is a privacy consideration, not evidence of malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Raffle drawings can be hard to verify when entries live in lists or spreadsheets. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through clear random winner selection workflows.
actual_capabilities: Selects one or more random winners from plain lists, CSV files, Excel files, or Google Sheets.
Supports excluding previous winners to reduce duplicate selections.
Supports weighted selection when an entries column represents ticket counts.
Can pick runner-ups alongside primary winners.
Prompts for transparent result details such as row number, timestamp, and selection method.
Can help split participant lists into random teams.
limitations: It does not include executable code or a verifiable random number implementation.
Google Sheets and local file access depend on the host tool permissions.
It cannot verify eligibility unless the user provides clear contest rules and data.
Seeded verification is described in the skill, but no implementation is provided.

use_cases.title: Social Giveaway Winner
use_cases.description: Pick a winner from exported giveaway entries and record the selection details for public transparency.

use_cases.title: Event Raffle Drawing
use_cases.description: Select multiple prize winners from an attendee spreadsheet while avoiding duplicate winners.

use_cases.title: Research Participant Selection
use_cases.description: Randomly select survey participants or beta testers from a submitted list.

prompt_templates.title: Pick One Winner
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner raffle from a pasted list
prompt_templates.prompt: Pick one random winner from this list. Show the selected name, entry position, timestamp, and selection method.

prompt_templates.title: Pick Several Winners
prompt_templates.scenario: Multiple winners from a local spreadsheet
prompt_templates.prompt: Pick 5 random winners from entries.csv. Do not select the same person twice. Include row numbers and a short audit summary.

prompt_templates.title: Use Exclusions and Runner-Ups
prompt_templates.scenario: Contest with previous winners and backups
prompt_templates.prompt: Pick 1 winner and 3 runner-ups from this spreadsheet. Exclude previous winners listed in the Previous Winners column.

prompt_templates.title: Run Weighted Drawing
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a weighted raffle using the entries column as ticket count. Pick 2 winners, explain the weighting, and list the selected rows.

output_examples.input: Pick one winner from a pasted list of newsletter subscribers.
output_examples.output: Winner selected: Sarah Johnson. Entry position: 142. Selection method: random draw. Timestamp included for audit records.

output_examples.input: Pick three event raffle winners and two runner-ups from attendees.csv.
output_examples.output: The result lists three primary winners, two runner-ups, source row numbers, and a note that duplicate winners were excluded.

output_examples.input: Pick a weighted winner using the entries column in a contest spreadsheet.
output_examples.output: The result names the winner, shows the weighted ticket basis, and summarizes how the drawing used the entries column.

best_practices: Remove unnecessary personal data before sharing entry files with an AI tool.
Define eligibility rules and exclusions before running the drawing.
Save the timestamp, source file name, and selected row details for records.
anti_patterns: Do not rerun drawings until a preferred winner appears.
Do not mix eligible and ineligible entries in the same draw without clear labels.
Do not publish emails, phone numbers, or private entry details with winner announcements.

faq.question: Can it pick winners from Google Sheets?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill describes selecting winners from Google Sheets when the host tool has access to the sheet.

faq.question: Can it prevent duplicate winners?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes workflows for excluding previous winners and avoiding duplicate selections.

faq.question: Does it include executable raffle code?
faq.answer: No. The skill is an instruction file and does not include a runnable random selection implementation.

faq.question: Can it support weighted raffles?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes using an entries column where each entry count represents raffle tickets.

faq.question: Is it safe for personal data?
faq.answer: Use care. The workflow may process names and emails, so users should minimize and protect personal data.

faq.question: Can it pick runner-ups?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes prompts for selecting runner-ups after choosing the main winner.
